# npm audit report

postcss  <=8.5.17
Severity: high
PostCSS: Path Traversal in Previous Source Map Auto-Loading (sourceMappingURL) leads to Arbitrary .map File Disclosure - https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-r28c-9q8g-f849
fix available via `npm audit fix --force`
Will install next@9.3.3, which is a breaking change
node_modules/postcss
  next  >=9.3.4-canary.0
  Depends on vulnerable versions of postcss
  node_modules/next
    @opennextjs/aws  >=3.9.5
    Depends on vulnerable versions of next
    node_modules/@opennextjs/aws
      @opennextjs/cloudflare  0.3.0 - 0.6.6 || >=1.14.2
      Depends on vulnerable versions of @opennextjs/aws
      Depends on vulnerable versions of next
      node_modules/@opennextjs/cloudflare
    geist  >=1.0.1
    Depends on vulnerable versions of next
    node_modules/geist
    next-auth  <=0.0.0-pr.11562.ed0fce23 || >=4.11.0
    Depends on vulnerable versions of next
    node_modules/next-auth
    next-intl  <=0.0.0-package-boundaries-1 || >=0.0.2
    Depends on vulnerable versions of next
    node_modules/next-intl

7 high severity vulnerabilities

To address all issues (including breaking changes), run:
  npm audit fix --force
